title: Tangy Light Syrian Monkfish Crumble
description: A light and tangy date-night dish featuring monkfish in a paleo-friendly crumble sauce, perfect for those who prefer simple yet flavorful meals.

ingredients.name: Lemon juice
ingredients.notes: fresh squeezed, add more to taste

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: minced for a mild garlic flavor

ingredients.name: Herbs de Provence
ingredients.notes: for a touch of authenticity and flavor

instructions: Season the monkfish fillets with a pinch of salt and pepper on both sides.

Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the monkfish fillets, skin-side down, and cook for about 4-5 minutes or until golden brown. Flip and cook for another 3-4 minutes or until done to your liking (internal temperature should reach at least 63°C or 145°F). Remove from heat.

In a small bowl, mix together lemon juice, dried sumac, minced garlic, and herbs de Provence. Pour this mixture over the cooked monkfish fillets. Let it sit for about 2-3 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Arrange watercress leaves on plates as a garnish (if using). Serve hot with the monkfish fillets on top of the crumble sauce and the watercress leaves for extra freshness.
